nextPrompt.title: QA — Validation Tests for Malformed Metadata (E6-S1)
nextPrompt.rationale: Prompt 24.0.1 is next because: all 1 prerequisites are met, it has the lowest sequence number among 3 eligible prompts, and it unblocks 2 downstream prompt(s).
nextPrompt.body:
# Prompt 24.0.1: QA — Validation Tests for Malformed Metadata (E6-S1)

## Sequence Position

- Prompt: 24.0.1
- Epic: E6
- Story: E6-S1
- Tasks: E6-S1-T1 through T3
- Phase: Validation

## Prerequisites

- 23.0.1 complete (UI review approved)

## Context for the Agent

You are the QA Test Architect. Create comprehensive validation test suites for malformed metadata handling. Verify the parser handles missing fields, invalid types, empty frontmatter, and no frontmatter gracefully.

## Required Reading

```text
docs/test-strategy.md
docs/project-manager-dashboard-data-contract.md
agents/epics/project-manager-dashboard-epics.md
agents/handoffs/S-2026-04-04-026-review-gate-ui.md
```

## Scope

### Part A — Review Gate Findings (from 23.0.1)

Before starting E6-S1 validation work, address the following approved findings from the UI code review gate. These are pre-approved for implementation — no further review needed.

#### A1. Dead code removal
- Delete `src/lib/constants.ts` (duplicate `STATUS_THEME` never imported; canonical version is `src/constants/statusTheme.ts`)

#### A2. Low-coverage hardening
- Add tests for `Pagination.tsx` edge cases (ellipsis rendering, boundary pages) — target ≥80% lines
- Add tests for `useDrawer.ts` focus trap Tab cycling (forward Tab, Shift+Tab wrap, handleTab code paths) — target ≥80% lines
- Improve `fileWatcher.ts` test coverage for `createFileWatcher` start/stop logic — target ≥80% lines (mock chokidar integration paths)

#### A3. Missing chart
- Add "Remaining Prompts Over Time" line chart to the overview page (`completionTimeline` data already available from parser `SummaryMetrics`)

#### A4. Accessibility enhancements
- Add `prefers-reduced-motion` support: drawer slide-in animation should appear instantly when user prefers reduced motion
- Add CSP `<meta>` tag to `src/app/layout.tsx` restricting inline scripts and `eval()` (defense-in-depth per security review HIGH-002)

#### A5. Filter URL sync
- Sync filter state (status, epic, location, role, search) to URL query params on the Prompts and Sessions pages for shareability (per wireframe §8.6)

### Part B — Validation Tests for Malformed Metadata (E6-S1)

- Create test fixtures: missing fields, invalid types, empty frontmatter, no frontmatter, partial data
- Write tests verifying parser resilience (no crashes)
- Write tests verifying warning output for each invalid case
- Write tests verifying partial data extraction where possible
- Verify dashboard UI handles warnings display correctly

## Constraints

- Part A items are pre-approved for implementation — modify source code as needed
- Part B tests must use the test framework established in scaffolding (Jest)
- Part B: do not modify parser behavior — test existing behavior and file bugs if broken

## Validation Commands

```bash
cd apps/dashboard && npm test -- --testPathPattern="malformed|validation" --coverage
```

## Handoff Instructions

Create handoff notes. Include: test count, pass/fail results, bugs filed if any.


## Session Close-Out Checklist

Before committing, the executing agent MUST complete ALL of the following steps.
This checklist is mandatory for both local and cloud agents.

1. **Create session handoff** using `agents/templates/session-handoff-template.md`
   - Save to `agents/handoffs/S-<date>-<seq>-<slug>.md`

2. **Update this prompt's frontmatter:**
   - Set `status: "done"`
   - Set `session_handoff` to the handoff file path created in step 1
   - Set `completed_at` to the current ISO-8601 timestamp
   - Set `updated_at` to the current ISO-8601 timestamp

3. **Update `prompts/index.md`:**
   - Change this prompt's row: status to `done`, add handoff path, set completed date, add summary note
   - Update the summary counts (decrement ready, increment done)

4. **Update `agents/context/status-dashboard.md`:**
   - Add this prompt to Active Work Packets as **DONE** with a brief note
   - Update Metrics counts (Prompts Completed, ready/done tallies)
   - Update Next Suggested Action to the next prompt in sequence
   - Update Awaiting Review if any items were resolved or added

5. **Update `agents/context/decision-log.md`:**
   - Add a row for each decision made during this session

6. **Commit and push:**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "Complete prompt <prompt_id>: <title>"
   git push
   ```
## Downstream Consumers

- 25.0.1 (Performance tests)

## Stop Conditions

- STOP if the parser crashes on any test fixture (file a blocker bug)

stories.title: Define and validate prompt frontmatter contract
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: All prompt YAML frontmatter fields from the data contract are documented, including lifecycle fields (`session_handoff`, `completed_at`, `archived_at`, `supersedes`, `superseded_by`, `insert_reason`)

AC-3: Validator rejects files with no YAML frontmatter (error), accepts files with missing optional fields (warning)
AC-4: At least 3 example prompt files pass validation with zero errors and zero warnings

prompts.title: Product Manager — PRD Review and Gap Analysis
prompts.body:
# Prompt 1.0.1: Product Manager — PRD Review and Gap Analysis

## Sequence Position

- Prompt: 1.0.1
- Epic: E1 (Repo Data Contracts and Parsing Foundation) + all epics
- Story: Cross-cutting
- Tasks: N/A
- Phase: Product Definition

## Prerequisites

- None (first prompt in the sequence)

## Context for the Agent

You are the Product Manager. Review the PRD, data contract, and epics for the Project Manager Dashboard. Identify gaps, ambiguities, unstated assumptions, missing acceptance criteria, and conflicting requirements. Produce a structured gap analysis document.

## Required Reading

```text
docs/project-manager-dashboard-prd.md
docs/project-manager-dashboard-data-contract.md
agents/epics/project-manager-dashboard-epics.md
agents/context/project-charter.md
agents/context/product-brief.md
agents/context/assumptions.md
agents/context/constraints.md
```

## Scope

- Review all functional requirements (FR-1 through FR-16) for completeness
- Identify any user stories that lack clear acceptance criteria
- Flag requirements that conflict with constraints
- List any unstated assumptions
- Identify any missing non-functional requirements (performance, accessibility, etc.)
- Produce a structured gap analysis as a markdown document

## Constraints

- Do not rewrite the PRD — produce a review report
- Do not make stack or architecture decisions
- Do not generate code

## Validation Commands

```bash
# Verify gap analysis document was created
test -f docs/prd-gap-analysis.md
```

## Handoff Instructions

Create handoff notes using `agents/templates/session-handoff-template.md`. Include:
- List of identified gaps and ambiguities
- Recommended priority for resolution
- Any blocking issues that must be resolved before architecture review


## Session Close-Out Checklist

Before committing, the executing agent MUST complete ALL of the following steps.
This checklist is mandatory for both local and cloud agents.

1. **Create session handoff** using `agents/templates/session-handoff-template.md`
   - Save to `agents/handoffs/S-<date>-<seq>-<slug>.md`

2. **Update this prompt's frontmatter:**
   - Set `status: "done"`
   - Set `session_handoff` to the handoff file path created in step 1
   - Set `completed_at` to the current ISO-8601 timestamp
   - Set `updated_at` to the current ISO-8601 timestamp

3. **Update `prompts/index.md`:**
   - Change this prompt's row: status → `done`, add handoff path, set completed date, add summary note
   - Update the summary counts (decrement ready, increment done)

4. **Update `agents/context/status-dashboard.md`:**
   - Add this prompt to Active Work Packets as **DONE** with a brief note
   - Update Metrics counts (Prompts Completed, ready/done tallies)
   - Update Next Suggested Action to the next prompt in sequence

# Prompt 9.0.1: Architect — Technical Task Generation for Parser (E1)

## Sequence Position

- Prompt: 9.0.1
- Epic: E1 (Repo Data Contracts and Parsing Foundation)
- Story: E1-S1 through E1-S6
- Tasks: All E1 tasks
- Phase: Technical Task Generation

## Prerequisites

- 8.0.1 complete (Review gate approved)

## Context for the Agent

You are the Solution Architect. Break down all Epic E1 stories into detailed technical tasks with implementation specifications. Each task should include file paths, function signatures, interface definitions, and test requirements sufficient for an engineer to implement without ambiguity.

## Required Reading

```text
agents/epics/project-manager-dashboard-epics.md
agents/decisions/ADR-001-stack-selection.md
agents/decisions/ADR-002-parser-architecture.md
agents/context/architecture-overview.md
docs/test-strategy.md
docs/business-rules.md
agents/handoffs/S-2026-04-04-011-pre-implementation-review-gate.md
```

## Scope

- For each E1 task, specify: file paths, function/module design, input/output contracts, error handling, test cases
- Define the parser module folder structure
- Define the TypeScript interfaces for all parsed models
- Define the CLI entry point specification
- Produce technical task specifications as a markdown document

### ADR-002 Conditions Resolution (Required)

The 8.0.1 review gate issued a GO with conditions: all 6 ADR-002 conditions **must** be resolved in this prompt before E1 implementation begins. The technical task document must explicitly address each:

1. **`prompts/index.md` as canonical source (ADR-002 §1):** Specify the two-phase parsing approach — (a) parse `prompts/index.md` for the canonical prompt registry, (b) scan `prompts/active/` and `prompts/archive/` for frontmatter details. Folder contents supplement but do not override the index inventory. Reference `docs/business-rules.md` Section 4 for file discovery order.

2. **`ParseWarning` interface (ADR-002 §2):** Define the `ParseWarning` interface that `DashboardState.warnings` references. Minimum shape: `{ file: string; line?: number; code: string; message: string; severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info' }`. Align warning codes with the error taxonomy in `docs/business-rules.md`.

3. **Missing `DashboardState` interfaces (ADR-002 §3, Risk R12):** Define `ProjectSummary`, `SummaryMetrics`, and `NextPromptInfo` interfaces referenced in `DashboardState`. Derive field specifications from the data contract and `docs/business-rules.md` counting/rollup rules.

4. **Prompt lifecycle fields (ADR-002 §4, Risk R10):** Add missing fields to the `ParsedPrompt` interface: `sessionHandoff`, `supersedes`, `supersededBy`, `insertReason`, `completedAt`, `archivedAt`. These are required for FR-6 (prompt inventory) and FR-7 (prompt detail drawer). Reference the data contract Section 1.1.

5. **Derived metrics computation (ADR-002 §5):** Document which metrics the Graph Builder layer computes (completion rollups, status counts, dependency resolution) vs. which are deferred to the UI layer. Reference `docs/business-rules.md` Section 2 for counting and rollup rules.

6. **Epic/story/task parsing schema (ADR-002 §6, Risk R11):** Define formal parsing rules for extracting epic → story → task hierarchy from `agents/epics/*.md` files. Reference `docs/business-rules.md` Section 4 for the combined markdown format specification.

## Constraints

- Do not write implementation code — produce specifications
- Focus on what each task must produce, not how to code it

## Validation Commands

```bash
test -f docs/technical-tasks-e1-parser.md
```

## Handoff Instructions

sessions.summary: Executed kickoff prompt for Project Manager Dashboard. Reviewed PRD, data contract, and epics. Identified gaps and assumptions. Expanded 6 epics into 24 stories with 87 technical tasks and acceptance criteria. Proposed 3 ADRs (stack: Next.js+TypeScript+Chart.js, parser: three-layer pipeline, UI: five-view SPA). Generated 30 sequential prompts covering product definition, cross-functional review, technical task generation, implementation, validation, and release. Updated all context documents. Created agents/decisions/ and agents/handoffs/ directories. Repo is ready for sequential prompt execution starting with 1.0.1.

Implemented prompt lifecycle governance: created folder structure (active/archive/draft/generated/templates), migrated 34 prompts to active/, created lifecycle standard, updated numbering standard with superseding rules, updated prompt and handoff templates with lifecycle metadata, created canonical inventory (prompts/index.md), updated PRD and data contract for lifecycle awareness, updated all 30 dashboard prompt frontmatters, cleaned up staging artifacts.

Completed PRD review and gap analysis for the Project Manager Dashboard. Identified 11 functional requirement gaps, 3 user story gaps, 4 data contract gaps, 3 constraint conflicts, 5 unstated assumptions, 7 missing NFRs, and 4 epic/story-level gaps. Prioritized findings into three tiers: must-resolve before architecture (5 items), should-resolve before implementation (8 items), can-resolve during implementation (8+ items). Key blocking issues: epic/story/task data contract mismatch with actual file format, missing JSON schema spec, time-series chart data source infeasibility, and missing prompts/index.md parsing story.

Created comprehensive business rules document (docs/business-rules.md) covering 10 sections: status model with transition rules, completion rollup formulas, next-prompt selection algorithm with rationale text format, parser behavior rules, repo capability detection, chart data source rules, search/filter rules, multi-repo selector rules, project health signal, and auto-refresh scope. Refined acceptance criteria for all 24 stories across 6 epics with numbered, testable AC items totaling 120+ criteria. Resolved all 30+ PM gap analysis items. Added 5 new assumptions (A11–A15).

Created comprehensive text/ASCII wireframe descriptions for all five dashboard views (Overview, Epics, Prompts, Sessions, Tasks). Defined navigation flow and cross-view linking patterns. Documented full accessibility requirements including keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, color contrast, and motion preferences. Identified 10 UX concerns with actionable recommendations.

Reviewed all three ADRs against the PRD, data contract, epics, constraints, and assumptions. ADR-001 (Stack Selection) and ADR-003 (UI Architecture) approved unconditionally. ADR-002 (Parser Architecture) approved with conditions: must add prompts/index.md two-phase parsing, define ParseWarning interface, add missing lifecycle fields to ParsedPrompt, and clarify epic markdown parsing format. Architecture overview fully rewritten with comprehensive system design. Risk register expanded with 6 new risks (R9–R14), R8 mitigated.

Generated 55 technical task specifications across 16 stories in Epics E2–E5. Defined UI component folder structure, page/route structure, shared component contracts (StatusBadge, ProgressBar, ErrorBoundary, FilterBar, CopyButton, Pagination), state management (DashboardContext + hooks), and detailed specifications for all E2–E5 tasks including props interfaces, file paths, data flow, and test requirements.

Built Overview view summary cards (SummaryCard, HealthBadge, OverallProgressBar, SummaryCardsGrid) and chart panels (EpicCompletionChart bar, PromptStatusChart doughnut, SessionThroughputChart line). Wired page to DashboardState with loading/error states. 50 component tests and 6 snapshots.

UI Code Review Gate completed. All four reviewer perspectives (Architect, QA, Security, UX) evaluated the implementation across prompts 16.0.1–22.0.1. APPROVED with no critical or blocking findings. 3 low-priority observations documented for 24.0.1 hardening.
